prompt: The image write silently fails to save. What is the bug?
choices: ImageIO.write throws IOException when the format does not match the file extension

The format string "jpeg" causes the image to be encoded as JPEG data and written to the file regardless of extension; the file will contain JPEG data with a .png name — if any registered JPEG writer exists, ok will be true but the file is misnamed
ImageIO.write returns false when no writer is registered for the given format string; "jpeg" is not a valid format identifier — use "jpg" or "png"
BufferedImage.TYPE_INT_RGB cannot be saved as JPEG because JPEG requires TYPE_INT_BGR
explanation: ImageIO.write returns false when no ImageWriter is registered for the specified format string. The valid format name for JPEG is "jpg" (or "JPEG" depending on the JDK), not "jpeg". Fix: use "jpg" or "png" as the format string, and match the file extension accordingly.
